summary: Comprehensive testing of Element Diameter Q-Factor Physics Model fix. All 20 backend tests passed (100%). The fix is VERIFIED: element diameter now properly affects bandwidth and SWR via the thickness parameter Ω = 2·ln(2L/a). Fat elements (1.25") give wider bandwidth (0.946 MHz) and lower SWR (1.94). Thin elements (0.25") give narrower bandwidth (0.739 MHz) and higher SWR (2.71). Reference 0.5" gives bandwidth 0.816 MHz, SWR 2.37. Regression tests for gamma-fine-tune and optimize-return-loss all passed.

minor.issue: Description threshold for 'thin' elements is bandwidth_mult < 0.9, but 0.25" gives 0.906 which shows 'standard' description. This is a cosmetic issue only - physics is working correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: Fix VERIFIED: antenna_q is no longer hardcoded to 12.0, now computed as 12.0 * dia_q_info['q_ratio']
compute_diameter_q_factor() at ~line 266 correctly calculates Q from thickness parameter Ω = 2·ln(2L/a)
calculate_swr_from_elements() at ~line 320 now uses Q for SWR sensitivity scaling
bandwidth section at ~line 1335 correctly applies dia_q_info['bandwidth_mult'] to bandwidth calculation
element_q_info is correctly returned in AntennaOutput with all required fields: q_ratio, bandwidth_mult, swr_curve_exponent, description
Expected relationships verified: 1.25" q_ratio=0.86 (expected ~0.86), 0.5" q_ratio=1.0 (reference), 0.25" q_ratio=1.10 (expected ~1.10)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Element diameter Q-factor physics is fully working. Key test file: /app/backend/tests/test_diameter_q_factor.py. Verified relationships: Fat elements (1.25") give q_ratio ~0.86, bandwidth_mult ~1.16, wider bandwidth, lower SWR. Thin elements (0.25") give q_ratio ~1.10, bandwidth_mult ~0.91, narrower bandwidth, higher SWR. Reference 0.5" has q_ratio=1.0, bandwidth_mult=1.0.

POST /api/calculate response contains el.evidence: All 5-element test responses include element_q_info with q_ratio, bandwidth_mult, swr_curve_exponent, description

POST /api/gamma-fine-tune regression.evidence: Endpoint returns 200, optimized_elements differ from input, SWR improves

diameter_1.25_inch.description: Fat elements — wide bandwidth, shallow SWR curve
